content.problemStatement:
Teams AAA and BBB are having a penalty shoot-out to decide the winner of their football match. Each team gets to take a shot NNN times, and the team with the higher number of scored goals at the end wins. If the number of goals scored during the shoot-out by each team turns out to be the same even after all 2N2N2N shots, then they stop and agree that the result is a draw.

The two teams take shots alternately — team AAA shoots first, then team BBB shoots, then team AAA and so on until team BBB takes its NNN-th shot (at which point each team has taken exactly NNN shots). Even though all 2N2N2N shots are taken, the result of the shoot-out is often known earlier — e.g. if team AAA has already scored N−1N-1N−1 goals and team BBB has missed at least two shots, team AAA is definitely the winner.

For each shot, you know whether it was a goal or a miss. You need to find the earliest moment when the winner is known — the smallest sss (0≤s≤2N0 \le s \le 2N0≤s≤2N) such that after sss shots, the result of the shoot-out (whether team AAA won, team BBB won or the match is drawn) would be known even if we did not know the results of the remaining 2N−s2N-s2N−s shots.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a string SSS with length 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N, where for each valid iii, the iii-th character is '0' if the iii-th shot was a miss or '1' if it was a goal.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the smallest sss such that after sss shots, the result of the shoot-out is known.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: After four shots, team AAA has scored 222 goals and team BBB has scored 000 goals. Whatever happens afterwards, team AAA is guaranteed to win, since even if team BBB scored their last (and only) goal and team AAA did not score their last goal, team AAA would still win by 111 goal.
Example case 2: Team AAA scores the first goal and after that, neither team scores any goals until the last shot. Up till then, there is always a possibility that the match could end in a draw if team BBB scored a goal with its last shot, or in the victory of team AAA if it was a miss. We can only guarantee the result after the last shot.
